claim: We are in a recursive self-improvement phase where AI agents help build the next version of themselves, creating an intelligence explosion
actionable: Design agent systems with feedback loops where agents improve their own training data, harnesses, and configurations based on experiment results.

claim: Minimax M2.7 handles 30-50% of the model development workflow autonomously, with agents updating memory, building skills, and improving learning processes
actionable: Apply autonomous loops to your research workflow: have agents propose experiments, run them, analyze results, and suggest next steps with minimal human intervention.

claim: OpenAI's GPT-5.3 Codex was instrumental in creating itself, with early versions debugging the training and managing deployment of later versions
actionable: Use frontier models to improve themselves: iterate checkpoints autonomously, using early models to debug and optimize later training runs.

claim: Andrej Karpathy's Auto Research system uses frontier models to autonomously design, run, and iterate ML experiments on feature branches without human involvement
actionable: Use Karpathy's Auto Research pattern: point a frontier model at a task (e.g., optimize GPT-2 training), let it propose and run experiments in a loop, accumulating improvements on a git branch.

claim: Anthropic has been using Claude Code for autonomous loops where the agent writes code, runs tests, and iterates continuously on problems
actionable: Enable autonomous feedback loops in Claude Code by setting abstract problems, letting it iterate with tests, and reviewing solutions periodically.

claim: Google's AlphaEvolve discovered faster matrix multiplication for the first time in 50 years by autonomously improving system-wide architecture, saving billions of dollars
actionable: Run autonomous optimization agents on your system architecture; recursive improvement can unlock fundamentally better algorithms that compound across all downstream usage.
